message.content:
Repo: /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-1). Venv (run PoCs): /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
This is a dataset-agnostic ML/methodology engine being hardened for PRODUCTION. Hard constraints (do NOT flag as bugs): local-first/data-sovereign (loopback only, nothing leaves the box), no metered API (subscription OAuth only), engine never imports adapters/datasets (ADR-0002), optional heavy backends are lazy-imported (ADR-0006). Read real code; prefer findings you can back with a concrete repro/PoC against the venv. Report ONLY genuine production-readiness defects (not style/nits). Each finding needs a real file path and, where possible, a line.

You are independent VERIFIER #2 of 2. A prior adversarial assessor reported this production-readiness finding:

TITLE: Failed runs are terminated in the experiment tracker as FINISHED — a failed run is indistinguishable from a successful one in the metric store
DIMENSION: observability & provenance completeness
CLAIMED SEVERITY: MEDIUM
FILE: /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py 522-527 (finally) and tracking.py:84-85 (end_run)
DESCRIPTION: run_study's finally block calls tracker.end_run(run_id) on every exit path, including when a phase raised. MlflowExperimentTracker.end_run calls client.set_terminated(run_id) with no status argument, and MLflow's set_terminated defaults status to "FINISHED" (confirmed from the installed mlflow: the signature is set_terminated(run_id, status=None, ...) and the docstring states it "Defaults to FINISHED"). Result: a run that aborted on a firewall violation or a blocked gate is recorded in the on-box experiment store with status FINISHED, identical to a clean completion. Operators querying the tracking store (the canonical place to see which runs succeeded) cannot tell failed runs from successful ones, and partial metrics logged before the failure sit under a run that looks complete. This defeats the tracker's role as a production run-status surface.
SUGGESTED FIX: Distinguish success from failure: capture the outcome in run_study (e.g. set an `ok` flag true only after trail.verify() succeeds) and pass a status into end_run. Add a status parameter to ExperimentTracker.end_run / MlflowExperimentTracker.end_run (default 'FINISHED', pass 'FAILED' on the exception path) so set_terminated(run_id, status=status) marks aborted runs FAILED. The set_terminated call already tolerates the 'KILLED'/'FAILED' RunStatus values.

Independently determine whether this is a TRUE production-readiness problem. Read the actual code; attempt a concrete PoC against the venv if applicable. Set is_real=false if it is a false positive, already-handled, or merely a documented/justified design choice given the hard constraints. Recalibrate severity. Provide your evidence (what you read/ran).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""Experiment tracking — a local, on-box run/metric/artifact store (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's MLflow setup. The backend is a local store
4	(default: a SQLite file under the working directory); a remote tracking server is rejected by
5	the local-URI guard. MLflow is optional (the ``observability`` extra) and imported lazily, so
6	this module loads without it.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import os
12	from pathlib import Path
13	from typing import Any, Mapping, Protocol, runtime_checkable
14	
15	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
16	
17	_DEFAULT_URI = "sqlite:///assay_mlflow.db"
18	EXPERIMENT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_EXPERIMENT", "assay")
19	
20	
21	def get_tracking_uri() -> str:
22	    """Resolve the tracking URI (env override, else local SQLite), enforced local."""
23	    uri = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", _DEFAULT_URI)
24	    # Resolve a relative sqlite path to an absolute file so runs land deterministically.
25	    prefix = "sqlite:///"
26	    if uri.startswith(prefix):
27	        rel = uri[len(prefix) :]
28	        if not os.path.isabs(rel):
29	            uri = prefix + str((Path.cwd() / rel).resolve())
30	    return require_local_uri(uri, what="experiment tracking")
31	
32	
33	@runtime_checkable
34	class ExperimentTracker(Protocol):
35	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str: ...
36	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None: ...
37	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None: ...
38	    def end_run(self, run_id: str) -> None: ...
39	
40	
41	class MlflowExperimentTracker:
42	    """A local-store experiment tracker backed by MLflow (lazy import).
43	
44	    Construction validates the tracking URI is local and selects the experiment; it does not
45	    import MLflow until a run is started, so the module is importable without the extra.
46	    """
47	
48	    def __init__(self, experiment: str = EXPERIMENT_NAME) -> None:
49	        self._experiment = experiment
50	        self._uri = get_tracking_uri()  # validates local before anything else
51	
52	    def _client(self) -> Any:
53	        """An MlflowClient bound to the local store. Uses the client API (keyed by run_id)
54	        throughout, so no run ever sits on the global active-run stack — start_run followed by
55	        log_metric cannot collide (audit issue #O1)."""
56	        try:
57	            from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient
58	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
59	            raise RuntimeError(
60	                "experiment tracking requires the 'observability' extra (mlflow) — not installed"
61	            ) from exc
62	        return MlflowClient(tracking_uri=self._uri)
63	
64	    def _experiment_id(self, client: Any) -> str:
65	        exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
66	        if exp is not None:
67	            return str(exp.experiment_id)
68	        return str(client.create_experiment(self._experiment))
69	
70	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
71	        client = self._client()
72	        run = client.create_run(self._experiment_id(client), run_name=name)
73	        run_id = str(run.info.run_id)
74	        for k, v in dict(params).items():
75	            client.log_param(run_id, k, v)
76	        return run_id
77	
78	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None:
79	        self._client().log_metric(run_id, key, value)
80	
81	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None:
82	        self._client().log_artifact(run_id, path)
83	
84	    def end_run(self, run_id: str) -> None:
85	        self._client().set_terminated(run_id)
86

---DOC---
Set a run's status to terminated.

Args:
    run_id: The ID of the run to terminate.
    status: A string value of :py:class:`mlflow.entities.RunStatus`. Defaults to "FINISHED".
    end_time: If not provided, defaults to the current time.

content.text: MLflow confirmed: `set_terminated` defaults status to "FINISHED". Let me now do a concrete PoC to demonstrate that a failed run ends up FINISHED.

import sys

from assay_engine.observability.tracking import MlflowExperimentTracker

tr = MlflowExperimentTracker(experiment='poc')
# Simulate a run that fails partway: start, log a partial metric, then 'fail'
rid = tr.start_run('failing-run', {'mode':'discovery'})
tr.log_metric(rid, 'discovery_supported', 3.0)
# pipeline finally block: end_run regardless of failure
tr.end_run(rid)

# Now read back the status
from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient
c = MlflowClient(tracking_uri='sqlite:////tmp/assay_poc.db')
info = c.get_run(rid).info
print('RUN STATUS AFTER FAILURE PATH:', info.status)

content.text: PoC confirms a failed run is recorded as FINISHED. Let me verify the `track` helper and the failure semantics — specifically that exceptions propagate through the `finally` (so the run truly is a failure) and check whether there's any status differentiation anywhere.

content.text:
I have confirmed the full picture. The `try`/`finally` block (line 269-527) wraps all phases. On any raised exception (FirewallViolation, GateError, etc.), the `finally` block calls `tracker.end_run(run_id)`, which calls `set_terminated` with no status, defaulting to FINISHED. The exception then propagates (it is not swallowed), so the run genuinely failed but is recorded as FINISHED.

The finding is accurate and reproducible. Let me assess severity in context.

Key considerations:
- This is an observability/provenance defect, not a correctness or security defect. The provenance trail (`trail`) is the canonical, cryptographically-verified record (GOVERNANCE §3), and it DOES capture failures via `trail.record("tracking_error",...)` and the exception itself. The MLflow tracker is explicitly "best-effort" secondary observability.
- However, the tracker is positioned as the run-status/metric surface, and a FAILED run showing FINISHED with partial metrics is genuinely misleading to an operator querying the store. Partial metrics under a "FINISHED" run is a real provenance-completeness gap for production.
- The fix is trivial and idiomatic (MLflow natively supports FAILED status). MLflow itself uses FAILED on its own fluent-API exception path.

This is a real but bounded issue. The provenance trail mitigates total loss of failure information, but the tracker — the dedicated run-status store — does misreport. MEDIUM is slightly high given the mitigating provenance trail; I'll set it to LOW-MEDIUM. Given a real, reproducible operator-facing misreport in a production observability surface, but with the authoritative provenance trail correctly recording failure, I'll calibrate to LOW.

Let me double-check the provenance trail actually records the failure on the exception path (to confirm the mitigation).

273	            try:
274	                corpus = defn.parser.parse(plan.source)
275	                source_fp = defn.parser.source_fingerprint(plan.source)
276	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — normalize adapter/IO ingestion failures
277	                raise IngestionError(f"parser failed to ingest {plan.source!r}: {exc}") from exc
278	            if not corpus.units:
279	                raise IngestionError("ingestion produced an empty corpus")
280	            cfp = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
281	            source_version: str | None = None
282	            if versioner is not None:
283	                source_version = versioner.put(str(plan.source))
284	            trail.record(
285	                "ingest",
286	                "parsed source into canonical corpus",
287	                source_fingerprint=source_fp,
288	                corpus_fingerprint=cfp,
289	                n_units=len(corpus.units),
290	                n_relations=len(corpus.relations),
291	                source_version=source_version,
292	            )
293	            # split ids must reference real corpus units (no silent partition drift, audit H4)
294	            if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
295	                assert plan.split is not None
296	                unit_ids = {u.unit_id for u in corpus.units}
297	                unknown = (set(plan.split.discovery_ids) | set(plan.split.confirm_ids)) - unit_ids
298	                if unknown:
299	                    raise FirewallViolation(
300	                        f"split references {len(unknown)} id(s) absent from the corpus "
301	                        f"(e.g. {sorted(unknown)[:3]}) — partition would silently drop units"
302	                    )

content.text:
Confirmed. On the exception path the run aborts with the exception propagating; the provenance trail records `tracking_error` for tracker failures but NOT a generic "run failed" record on the firewall/gate exception path — the exception itself is the record there. The MLflow tracker, however, unconditionally marks the run FINISHED.

I have enough to render a verdict.

input.reasoning:
The finding is technically accurate and I reproduced it. In /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py the entire phase body is wrapped in try/...finally (lines 269-527). The finally block (lines 522-527) calls tracker.end_run(run_id) on EVERY exit path, including when a phase raised (FirewallViolation, GateError, IngestionError, ValueError, or a failed trail.verify()). The exception is NOT swallowed by the finally — it propagates — so the run genuinely failed. MlflowExperimentTracker.end_run (observability/tracking.py:84-85) calls self._client().set_terminated(run_id) with no status. I confirmed against the installed mlflow in the venv that set_terminated(run_id, status=None, end_time=None) defaults status to "FINISHED" (docstring: 'Defaults to "FINISHED"'). I then ran an end-to-end PoC using the engine's own MlflowExperimentTracker against a temp SQLite store: started a run, logged a partial metric, called end_run on the failure path, and read back info.status == "FINISHED". So a failed/aborted run with partial metrics is recorded in the on-box tracking store identically to a clean completion, and the suggested fix (add a status param, pass "FAILED" on the exception path) is correct and natively supported by mlflow.

I downgrade severity from MEDIUM to LOW for two reasons rooted in the codebase's stated design. (1) The MLflow tracker is explicitly and repeatedly documented as best-effort, secondary observability — start_run/log_metric failures are caught and never abort a run (track() at lines 223-230; start_run guarded at 257-267; end_run guarded at 523-527). The canonical, authoritative run record is the cryptographically-verified append-only provenance trail (GOVERNANCE §3, trail.verify() at line 521), which is returned in StudyResult.provenance and which does record the failure: the propagating exception is itself the failure signal and tracking errors are recorded as 'tracking_error' entries. So failure information is NOT lost system-wide; only the secondary metric-store status field is misleading. (2) Impact is confined to an operator eyeballing the MLflow store directly; there is no correctness, security, data-sovereignty, or methodological-firewall impact, and no hard constraint is involved. It is a genuine production observability/provenance-completeness defect (misreported run status + orphaned partial metrics under a 'FINISHED' run), worth fixing with the trivial proposed change, but bounded.
input.evidence: Read /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py: try at line 269, finally at 522-527 calling tracker.end_run(run_id) on all exit paths; trail.verify() at 521 inside the try (so a verify failure also hits the finally); track() best-effort wrapper lines 223-230; start_run best-effort lines 257-267. Read /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py lines 84-85: end_run -> self._client().set_terminated(run_id) with no status arg; module docstring (lines 1-7) states the tracker is optional/best-effort. Venv check (/Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python): inspect.signature(MlflowClient.set_terminated) == (self, run_id, status=None, end_time=None); docstring confirms status 'Defaults to "FINISHED"'. End-to-end PoC using the engine's own MlflowExperimentTracker against sqlite:////tmp/assay_poc.db: started 'failing-run', logged discovery_supported=3.0, called end_run, then MlflowClient.get_run(rid).info.status returned 'FINISHED' — a failed-path run indistinguishable from success, with partial metrics attached.

content.text:
Verified the finding. It is a real but low-severity production observability defect.

Summary: In `/Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py` the `finally` block (lines 522-527) calls `tracker.end_run(run_id)` on every exit path, and `MlflowExperimentTracker.end_run` in `/Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py` (lines 84-85) calls `set_terminated(run_id)` with no status. I confirmed in the venv that mlflow's `set_terminated` defaults status to "FINISHED", and reproduced end-to-end (via the engine's own tracker) that a failed-path run is recorded as FINISHED with partial metrics attached.

I downgraded MEDIUM to LOW because the tracker is explicitly best-effort secondary observability; the authoritative failure record is the cryptographically-verified provenance trail (which does capture the failure via the propagating exception and `tracking_error` entries). No correctness, security, or methodological-firewall impact, and no hard constraint is implicated. The proposed fix (add a `status` param, pass "FAILED" on the exception path) is correct and natively supported by mlflow.
